default.headline: Phone Bank with Common Cause!
default.description:
Join Common Cause for an important phone bank as we mobilize voters to take action against dangerous legislation in state houses and Congress. We will focus on the most urgent campaigns that need our help. Some of these campaigns may include stopping bills that will make it harder for millions of Americans—especially students, seniors, and veterans—to cast their ballots. We need your help to ensure voters across the country call their elected representatives and make their voices heard.

No prior experience is necessary—our team will provide full training at the start of each shift!

Colorado.headline: Join the Colorado Democracy Defense Coalition
Colorado.description: The Colorado Democracy Defense Coalition is a diverse alliance of Coloradans who are dedicated to safeguarding the 2026 election, watchdogging the process, supporting and assisting voters, and countering anti-voter extremism.

Florida.headline: Democracy Seminar with Common Cause Florida (Lunch & Learn)
Florida.description:
Our ongoing series of Florida Democracy Seminars provide a space where we can come together and learn how to fight back against voter suppression, the pernicious influence of money in politics, and other attacks on our freedom. By understanding changing laws and policies, our rights as Floridians, and the resources available to us, we strengthen our collective ability to ensure the promise of democracy for all Floridians.

When we show up informed and empowered, we win.

Georgia.headline: Georgia Action Team
Georgia.description: The Georgia Action Team is a growing community of engaged Common Cause Georgia members, volunteers, and community members committed to protecting and strengthening democracy across our state.

Indiana.headline: Sign Our Petition: End ICE Detention at Miami Correctional Facility
Indiana.description: Join Common Cause Indiana as we urge Governor Braun to end ICE detention at the Miami Correctional Facility.

Maryland.headline: Phone Bank with Common Cause!
Maryland.description:
We are showing up together again on March 28.

When our families are under attack and costs are pushing people to the brink, silence is not an option. We will defend ourselves and our communities against this administration’s unjust and cruel acts of violence. America does not belong to strongmen, greedy billionaires, or those who rule through fear. It belongs to us, the people.

Massachusetts.headline: Email your lawmakers: It's time for Same Day Registration
Massachusetts.description: State lawmakers need to hear from you about our priority voting rights reform. Contact them today!

Michigan.headline: Michigan Phonebank
Michigan.description:
We're looking for passionate individuals to help us spread the word and recruit volunteers for our upcoming events and confirm them for events they have registered for!

By volunteering your time to make calls, you'll play a crucial role in growing our community and ensuring our events are a success. It's an easy and impactful way to give back and help others get involved. Whether you're a first-time caller or a seasoned pro, your efforts will make a difference.

No prior experience is necessary—our team will provide full training at the start of each shift!

Nebraska.headline: Protect Nebraska’s Electoral Advantage
Nebraska.description: Nebraskans across the state are speaking out - please write a letter to the editor to tell your senators that our votes matter and LR24CA must fail.

New Mexico.headline: Help Protect the Vote in New Mexico!
New Mexico.description:
Election Protection is a nonpartisan voter support program that helps ensure every eligible voter can successfully cast their ballot and have their voice heard. Volunteers help voters navigate the voting process, understand their rights, and access trusted election information before and during elections.

Election Protection volunteers support this work through voter outreach, hotline support, community education, and assistance at polling locations. This program is also an opportunity to build community, stay civically engaged, and join a statewide network of volunteers working together to strengthen democracy in New Mexico.

New York.headline: Demand Rank Choice Elections Statewide
New York.description: Implementing Ranked Choice Voting in Erie County and Buffalo will take all of us speaking up, organizing, and showing our elected leaders that we’re ready for a better method of voting.

North Carolina.headline: North Carolina Member Call
North Carolina.description: Common Cause NC is hosting a virtual call on May 14, 2026 at 6:00 p.m., where we will discuss our collective response to the State Board of Elections proposed anti-voter rules and the U.S. Supreme Court’s Callais decision.

Ohio.headline: Coffee with Common Cause Ohio
Ohio.description: Join Catherine Turcer and the Common Cause Ohio team to catch up on what's been going on in Ohio and learn how to take action. Grab your morning beverage and join the (virtual) conversation!

Oregon.headline: Join Oregon's Election Protection Team!
Oregon.description: Sign up to join Oregon's Election Protection Team as we spearhead numerous activities throughout the state to ensure our elections are fair and free of any tampering by the Trump administration.

Pennsylvania.headline: Watchdogs for Democracy: County Election Monitoring Interest Form
Pennsylvania.description:
Please fill out and submit this form if you are interested in helping Common Cause Pennsylvania monitor county election administrations across the state:

Here in Pennsylvania, decisions that determine how easy or hard it is to vote are made at the county level. We are actively recruiting volunteers to be watchdogs for democracy at the local level.

Rhode Island.headline: Send a Letter: The General Assembly Must Pass the RI Voting Rights Act!
Rhode Island.description: The Rhode Island Voting Rights Act was introduced in the Senate Judiciary Committee and the House State Government and Elections Committee. Now, the bill must be voted out of each committee and advanced to the floor of the House and the Senate. Use this easy tool to contact your legislators and ask them to support the RIVRA!

Texas.headline: Good Trouble Weekend of Action
Texas.description:
This July, six years after the passing of civil rights hero Congressman Lewis, we will continue the fight for voting rights with “Good Trouble Lives On Weekend of Action (GTLO).”
This year’s GTLO is a three-day national mobilization to honor the legacy of Congressman John Lewis by doing what he loved most - organizing, acting, and registering voters.
Join us by pledging to take action the week of July 17th!

Wisconsin.headline: Tell your state senator: Make voting more accessible in Wisconsin
Wisconsin.description: A.B. 312 already passed the State Assembly by voice vote last November but the Senate has not acted on it yet. We need to push the State Senate to pass this legislation and get it to Gov. Evers as soon as possible.
